name: Ventilation mode - Volume driven (VCV)
description: Volume-controlled ventilation modes — the ventilator delivers a set tidal volume.

concept.conceptName: Synchronized intermittent mandatory ventilation - volume control pressure-support inflation
Synchronized intermittent mandatory ventilation volume control inflation and an assured constant airway pressure adjunct during expiratory phase

en.name: Ventilation mode - Volume driven (VCV)
en.shortDescription: Volume-controlled ventilation modes — the ventilator delivers a set tidal volume.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Volume-controlled mechanical ventilation modes, where the ventilator delivers a set tidal volume and the resulting airway pressure varies with the patient's lung mechanics. In source data this is a **procedure** record indicating the ventilation mode in use; there is no unit. It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated patients. The mode is independent of whether ventilation is invasive (via an endotracheal/tracheostomy tube) or non-invasive (via a mask) — that distinction is captured in the Invasive mechanical ventilation and Non-invasive ventilation concept sets.

## Included Concepts

The set lists the SNOMED procedure concepts for these modes.

- *4055375 / 243150007 — Assisted controlled mandatory ventilation (SNOMED)*
- *4245036 / 59427005 — Synchronized intermittent mandatory ventilation (SNOMED)*
- *37152363 / 1186621003 — Synchronized intermittent mandatory ventilation - volume control pressure-support inflation (SNOMED)*
- *37152385 / 1186680000 — Synchronized intermittent mandatory ventilation volume control inflation and an assured constant airway pressure adjunct during expiratory phase (SNOMED)*
- *37152409 / 1186745009 — Assist control ventilation volume-control inflation-type (SNOMED)*

## Mapping Notes

- Map a recorded ventilation mode to the matching concept here based on the control variable / mode family, regardless of whether the patient is invasively or non-invasively ventilated.
- **Mode vs ventilation act**: this set captures the *mode* only; whether the patient is on invasive or non-invasive ventilation is recorded separately (Invasive mechanical ventilation / Non-invasive ventilation concept sets).

fr.name: Mode de ventilation - Piloté en volume (VCV)
fr.shortDescription: Modes de ventilation à volume contrôlé — le ventilateur délivre un volume courant réglé.

versions.message: Reorganize Ventilation mode subcategory: VCV holds volume-control modes. Added clinical description.
